hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 electrostatic interactions without compromising SSTR2 affinity, thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding but are not sufficiently differentiating from off-target SSTR3/SSTR5. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target binding while preserving pharmacophore positions.
These mutations are designed to break conserved hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets while maintaining flexibility and compatibility with SSTR2 pharmacophore.
